steps.title: Emphasize the shared effect
steps.content: The student wants a similarity between SEBS rubber and single-ply NiTi wire. The notes show that when each fiber was twisted, its average surface temperature increased.

steps.title: Avoid differences and context
steps.content: Choice A mentions both materials but does not state the shared heating effect. Choice C emphasizes that one material heated more than the other. Choice D defines torsional heating generally without focusing on the two materials. The remaining choice directly says both fibers heat up when twisted. The answer is B.
